info.name: Microsoft Azure Text Analytics Client
info.description: The Text Analytics API is a suite of natural language processing (NLP) services built with best-in-class Microsoft machine learning algorithms. The API can be used to analyze unstructured text for tasks such as sentiment analysis, key phrase extraction and language detection. Functionality for analysis of text specific to the healthcare domain and personal information are also available in the API. Further documentation can be found in https://docs.microsoft.com/en-us/azure/cognitive-services/text-analytics/overview Contact Support: Name: Microsoft Cognitive Services Email: mlapi@microsoft.com

item.name: Microsoft Azure Get Analysis Status And Results

query.description: (Optional) if set to true, response will contain request and document level statistics.
(Optional) Set the maximum number of results per task. When both $top and $skip are specified, $skip is applied first.
(Optional) Set the number of elements to offset in the response. When both $top and $skip are specified, $skip is applied first.

request.description: Get the status of an analysis job.  A job may consist of one or more tasks.  Once all tasks are completed, the job will transition to the completed state and results will be available for each task.

response.name: Analysis job status and metadata.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Submit Healthcare Analysis Job

query.description: (Optional) This value indicates which model will be used for scoring. If a model-version is not specified, the API should default to the latest, non-preview version.

(Optional) Specifies the method used to interpret string offsets.  Defaults to Text Elements (Graphemes) according to Unicode v8.0.0. For additional information see https://aka.ms/text-analytics-offsets

request.description: Start a healthcare analysis job to recognize healthcare related entities (drugs, conditions, symptoms, etc) and their relations.

response.name: Accepted - call results in a link where the status of the submitted job can be checked via the GET operation.

item.name: Microsoft Azure Entities Containing Personal Information

query.description: (Optional) This value indicates which model will be used for scoring. If a model-version is not specified, the API should default to the latest, non-preview version.
(Optional) if set to true, response will contain request and document level statistics.
(Optional) if specified, will set the PII domain to include only a subset of the entity categories. Possible values include: 'PHI', 'none'.

(Optional) Specifies the method used to interpret string offsets.  Defaults to Text Elements (Graphemes) according to Unicode v8.0.0. For additional information see https://aka.ms/text-analytics-offsets
(Optional) describes the PII categories to return

request.description: The API returns a list of entities with personal information (\"SSN\", \"Bank Account\" etc) in the document. For the list of supported entity types, check Supported Entity Types in Text Analytics API. See the Supported languages in Text Analytics API for the list of enabled languages.

response.name: A successful call results in a list of entities containing personal information returned for each valid document

request.description: The API returns the detected language and a numeric score between 0 and 1. Scores close to 1 indicate 100% certainty that the identified language is true. See the Supported languages in Text Analytics API for the list of enabled languages.

response.name: A successful call results in the detected language with the highest probability for each valid document

request.description: The API returns a detailed sentiment analysis for the input text. The analysis is done in multiple levels of granularity, start from the a document level, down to sentence and key terms (targets and assessments).

response.name: A successful call results in a document sentiment prediction, as well as sentiment scores for each sentiment class (Positive, Negative, and Neutral)
